calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Pamela Underwood, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: People of the State of California; no other appearances.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Petitioner People Of The State Of California's Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories; Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Pamela Underwood appearing for petitioner and moving party.
No appearance for respondent.

Ruling:
No argument was made. Tentative ruling adopted: Petitioner's Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner's First Set of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Form Interrogatories; First Set of Demand For Production and Inspection of Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Claimant is ordered to respond and to produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
